findings.text: She wasn't pushing north. She was kneeling at the nearest stone with frost worked through a chalk circle and notes all over the ground.
findings.deletionTest: She was kneeling at the nearest stone with frost worked through a chalk circle and notes all over the ground.
findings.reason: Preamble negation delays the committed image instead of stating the concrete action directly.
findings.suggestedFix: She had stopped at the nearest stone, kneeling with frost worked through a chalk circle and notes all over the ground.

findings.reason: The fragment chain rejects two labels before naming the real word, which is pure negation scaffolding.

findings.text: That the ward wasn't under attack. That it was carrying damage from farther south and nobody was listening.
findings.deletionTest: That it was carrying damage from farther south and nobody was listening.
findings.reason: The first sentence primes a discarded condition before the real claim arrives.
findings.suggestedFix: That it was carrying damage from farther south and nobody was listening.

findings.text: Angry. Not frightened, unless it was buried deep enough not to show.
findings.reason: The line swats away one descriptor before settling on the real emotional read.
findings.suggestedFix: Angry, with any fear buried too deep to show.

findings.text: Not all. Some on the road. Some before she crossed.
findings.deletionTest: Some on the road. Some before she crossed.
findings.reason: The opening negation is disposable scaffolding because the positive count survives cleanly without it.
findings.suggestedFix: Some on the road. Some before she crossed.

findings.text: She wasn't trying to run. She was furious we wouldn't let her keep working.
findings.deletionTest: She was furious we wouldn't let her keep working.
findings.reason: The negative setup only delays the real point, which is her fury at being interrupted.
findings.suggestedFix: She was furious we wouldn't let her keep working.

findings.text: No jewels. No velvet. No polished escort from the Kirin Tor. Just a hard-used young mage in a border cell, hands chewed raw by her own work, all of her bent toward the ward-stone instead of this room.
findings.deletionTest: Just a hard-used young mage in a border cell, hands chewed raw by her own work, all of her bent toward the ward-stone instead of this room.
findings.reason: The chain of refusals exists only to stage the real image that already lands on its own.
findings.suggestedFix: Just a hard-used young mage in a border cell, hands chewed raw by her own work, all of her bent toward the ward-stone instead of this room.

findings.text: She swung back into the saddle. This was the part of the world that made sense: bow across her back, road under hoof, a border that could be ridden and held by living hands instead of argued over by people who thought maps obeyed them.
findings.subject: maps
findings.realAgent: the officials reading and arguing over the border on paper
findings.reason: The clause launders human compliance onto maps instead of naming the people mistaking the documents for authority.
findings.suggestedFix: She swung back into the saddle. This was the part of the world that made sense: bow across her back, road under hoof, a border that could be ridden and held by living hands instead of argued over by people who mistook maps for authority.

findings.text: A budget session could wait until it learned to hunt.
findings.subject: A budget session
findings.realAgent: the Convocation members delaying field work with paperwork
findings.reason: The sentence gives a council session human learning and predatory intent instead of keeping the dismissal on the people running it.
findings.suggestedFix: A budget session could wait until after the patrol.

findings.text: On everything south of the city, unless the weather has learned respect.
findings.subject: the weather
findings.realAgent: the actual rough conditions south of the city
findings.reason: The line gives weather a human lesson instead of naming the worsening conditions directly.
findings.suggestedFix: On everything south of the city, unless the weather has worsened there too.

findings.text: The pleasant day died where it sat.
findings.subject: The pleasant day
findings.realAgent: the runner’s report ending the patrol ease
findings.reason: The sentence turns the day into an actor instead of naming the report that ended it.

findings.text: The space beside her opened at once.
findings.subject: The space beside her
findings.realAgent: Lor'themar's departure and Sylvanas's immediate awareness of it
findings.reason: Absence is made to act on its own instead of being felt by the POV character.
findings.suggestedFix: She felt Lor'themar's absence at once.

findings.text: The ward hum thickened until it sat in Sylvanas's teeth.
findings.subject: The ward hum
findings.realAgent: the vibration she could physically feel in her teeth
findings.reason: The hum is given body-like placement instead of the sensation being attached to Sylvanas directly.
findings.suggestedFix: The ward hum thickened until it buzzed in Sylvanas's teeth.

findings.subject: That
findings.realAgent: Sylvanas taking hold of the detail about Jaina’s hands
findings.reason: The abstract fact is treated as if it can settle itself rather than being registered by Sylvanas.

findings.text: It only made her stranger than expected, and strange problems liked to become personal if you left them alone.
findings.subject: strange problems
findings.realAgent: people letting unresolved threats escalate until they hit them personally
findings.reason: The sentence gives problems preference and intent instead of stating the escalation plainly.
findings.suggestedFix: It only made her stranger than expected, and strange problems became personal if you left them alone.

findings.text: Blue eyes fixed on Sylvanas at once.
findings.subject: Blue eyes
findings.realAgent: the prisoner looking straight at Sylvanas
findings.reason: The body-part subject steals volition from the prisoner even though the character is the real actor.
findings.suggestedFix: The prisoner fixed blue eyes on Sylvanas at once.

findings.text: Sylvanas marked it because she marked what guarded her lands.
findings.reason: This sentence carries the weakest mental uses of marked, and cutting them brings the chapter back toward the hard cap while preserving the stronger physical uses.
findings.suggestedFix: Sylvanas noted it because she tracked what guarded her lands.

summary.verdictReason: Pass A fails: multiple moderate/severe negation and intent-smear hits remain, including clustered fragment pivots and repeated agency laundering in narration and dialogue.
